description: Implement tiered contact matching pipeline during sync: handle match → exact name → fuzzy name with confidence-based merge suggestions

tasks.description: Extend merge source enum to support all tiers
steps: In packages/convex/convex/schema.ts, add 'exact_name_match' and 'fuzzy_name_match' to mergeSourceValidator
Verify: pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint - must pass

tasks.description: Export fuzzy-name module from @prm/ai contact-resolution index
steps: In packages/ai/src/contact-resolution/index.ts, add exports for nameSimilarity, namesMatch, NAME_MATCH_THRESHOLDS from fuzzy-name.ts
Verify: pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint - must pass

tasks.description: Create MatchResult type with confidence and source
steps: In packages/convex/convex/contactResolution.ts, define MatchResult type: { contactId: Id<'contacts'>, confidence: number, source: 'phone_match'|'email_match'|'exact_name_match'|'fuzzy_name_match', matchedValue?: string }
Update existing findBestMatch return type to MatchResult | null
Verify: pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint - must pass

tasks.description: Implement Tier 1: Handle matching (phone/email) with confidence 1.0
steps: In packages/convex/convex/contactResolution.ts, extract handle matching logic from findBestMatch into new function matchByHandle(contact1, contact2): MatchResult | null
Check emails first using emailsMatch from deterministic.ts, return {confidence: 1.0, source: 'email_match'}
Then check phones using phonesMatch from @prm/shared, return {confidence: 1.0, source: 'phone_match'}
Include matchedValue in result for audit trail
Verify: pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint - must pass

tasks.description: Implement Tier 2: Exact name matching with confidence 0.95
steps: In packages/convex/convex/contactResolution.ts, add function matchByExactName(contact1, contact2): MatchResult | null
Normalize both displayNames: lowercase, trim, remove extra spaces
If normalized names are equal and non-empty, return {confidence: 0.95, source: 'exact_name_match', matchedValue: normalizedName}
Skip if either contact has no displayName or displayName looks like phone/email (use existing looksLikePhone/looksLikeEmail)
Verify: pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint - must pass

tasks.description: Implement Tier 3: Fuzzy name matching with Jaro-Winkler scores
steps: In packages/convex/convex/contactResolution.ts, add function matchByFuzzyName(contact1, contact2): MatchResult | null
Import nameSimilarity, NAME_MATCH_THRESHOLDS from @prm/ai contact-resolution
Skip if either displayName looks like phone/email or is empty
Calculate similarity score using nameSimilarity(name1, name2)
If score >= NAME_MATCH_THRESHOLDS.MINIMUM (0.60), return {confidence: score, source: 'fuzzy_name_match', matchedValue: `${name1} ~ ${name2}`}
Verify: pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint - must pass

tasks.description: Integrate tiered matching into findBestMatch function
steps: In packages/convex/convex/contactResolution.ts, refactor findBestMatch to call tiers in order: matchByHandle → matchByExactName → matchByFuzzyName
Return first non-null result (highest priority tier wins)
Preserve backward compatibility: function signature unchanged
Verify: pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint - must pass

tasks.description: Update merge suggestion creation to use MatchResult source and confidence
steps: In packages/convex/convex/contactResolution.ts findDuplicateCandidatesInternal, pass match.source and match.confidence to createMergeSuggestion
In createMergeSuggestion internal mutation, accept source param from MatchResult instead of hardcoding
Priority formula already uses confidence: Math.round((1 - confidence) * 100) - verify this works for fuzzy scores
Verify: pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint - must pass

tasks.description: Add early exit optimization for handle-indexed matching
steps: In findDuplicateCandidatesInternal, after handle-index lookup returns candidates, run tier 1 (handle) matching first
Only run tier 2/3 (name matching) if no handle match found
This preserves O(n) complexity for most cases while adding name matching
Verify: pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint - must pass

tasks.description: Add unit tests for Tier 1 (handle matching)
steps: In packages/convex/convex/__tests__/contactResolution.test.ts, add test suite for matchByHandle
Test: email exact match returns confidence 1.0 and source 'email_match'
Test: phone variant match (10-digit vs 11-digit) returns confidence 1.0 and source 'phone_match'
Test: no match returns null
Run: cd packages/convex && pnpm test
Verify: pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint - must pass

tasks.description: Add unit tests for Tier 2 (exact name matching)
steps: In packages/convex/convex/__tests__/contactResolution.test.ts, add test suite for matchByExactName
Test: exact name match (case insensitive) returns confidence 0.95
Test: names with extra spaces normalize and match
Test: phone-like displayName skipped (returns null)
Test: email-like displayName skipped
Run: cd packages/convex && pnpm test
Verify: pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint - must pass

tasks.description: Add unit tests for Tier 3 (fuzzy name matching)
steps: In packages/convex/convex/__tests__/contactResolution.test.ts, add test suite for matchByFuzzyName
Test: 'Robert Smith' vs 'Bob Smith' returns score >= 0.60 (nickname expansion)
Test: 'John Doe' vs 'Jon Doe' returns score >= 0.80 (typo tolerance)
Test: completely different names return null (below 0.60)
Test: phone-like displayName skipped
Run: cd packages/convex && pnpm test
Verify: pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint - must pass

tasks.description: Add integration test for tiered pipeline priority
steps: In packages/convex/convex/__tests__/contactResolution.test.ts, add test for findBestMatch tier ordering
Test: contact with matching email AND fuzzy name returns email_match (tier 1 wins)
Test: contact with exact name AND fuzzy name returns exact_name_match (tier 2 wins)
Test: contact with only fuzzy name match returns fuzzy_name_match (tier 3)
Test: merge suggestion created with correct source field
Run: cd packages/convex && pnpm test
Verify: pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint - must pass
